parameters.name: genesis_search
parameters.description: Free-text search across GENESIS-Online (German official statistics) for tables, statistics, variables, time series and cubes. Returns codes (e.g. '12711-0001') that you can pass to genesis_table_metadata or genesis_get_table. NOTE: German search terms often return empty results — use language='en' with English terms (e.g. 'migration' instead of 'Wanderung') for best results.

value.category: ={{ $fromAI('category', 'One of: all, tables, statistics, variables, time-series, cubes (default: all)', 'string') }}

parameters.name: genesis_list_tables
parameters.description: List GENESIS tables filtered by code prefix. Examples: '12711*' for migration tables, '61*' for prices, '21*' for education, '23*' for health. Use '*' for everything.

value.selection: ={{ $fromAI('selection', 'Code prefix with wildcard, e.g. 12711* for migration', 'string') }}

parameters.name: genesis_list_statistics
parameters.description: Browse top-level GENESIS statistics (themes). Each statistic has a numeric code (e.g. '12711' for Wanderungsstatistik). Use the codes here as a prefix for genesis_list_tables.

value.selection: ={{ $fromAI('selection', 'Optional code prefix (e.g. 12* or empty for all)', 'string') }}

parameters.name: genesis_list_variables
parameters.description: List GENESIS variables (Merkmale) such as JAHR (year), NAT (nationality), GES (sex), DLAND (federal state). Optionally filter by code prefix or by type (klassifizierend, raeumlich, zeitlich, sachlich, insgesamt).

value.selection: ={{ $fromAI('selection', 'Optional code prefix (e.g. NAT* or empty for all)', 'string') }}
value.type: ={{ $fromAI('type', 'Alle | klassifizierend | raeumlich | zeitlich | sachlich | insgesamt (default: Alle)', 'string') }}

parameters.name: genesis_list_values
parameters.description: List concrete value keys of a GENESIS variable. E.g. name='NAT' returns ['NATD'=Deutsche, 'NATA'=Auslaender]; name='DLAND' returns federal-state codes. Use these keys as classifyingkey/regionalkey in genesis_get_table.

parameters.name: genesis_table_metadata
parameters.description: Get the full structure of a GENESIS table: variables in head/columns/rows, time range, last update timestamp. Always call this BEFORE genesis_get_table to know which classifying variables exist and how to filter.

parameters.name: genesis_get_table
parameters.description: Fetch the actual data of a GENESIS table as a CSV-like preview. Always narrow the result with startyear/endyear and filter variables — full tables are huge and the response is truncated at 4500 chars. To filter: pass regionalvariable + regionalkey (e.g. DLAND + 'DG' for Germany) or classifyingvariable1..3 + classifyingkey1..3 (e.g. NAT + 'DEUTSCH'). Use genesis_list_values to find valid keys.

value.name: ={{ $fromAI('name', 'Table code, e.g. 12711-0001', 'string') }}
value.regionalvariable: ={{ $fromAI('regionalvariable', 'Regional variable code (e.g. DLAND for federal states)', 'string') }}
value.regionalkey: ={{ $fromAI('regionalkey', 'Regional value key (e.g. DG for Germany total, BB for Brandenburg)', 'string') }}

parameters.name: genesis_get_timeseries
parameters.description: Fetch a single GENESIS time series by code (returned by genesis_search with category='time-series'). Optional year filter via startyear/endyear.

parameters.name: genesis_quality_signs
parameters.description: Reference: meaning of GENESIS quality codes used in data cells (e.g. '-' = no data, '/' = unreliable, '()' = limited validity, 'p' = preliminary). Use this when interpreting unusual values in genesis_get_table output.
